text: Meal Plans UTEP Business Affairs Meal Plans UTEP Business Affairs Meal Plans FY27 Meal Plans available for Purchase! With a variety of food options, the Pick 'n Shovel offers students an additional ch

he Pick 'n Shovel offers students an additional choice alongside national brands such as Starbucks and Jamba Juice. The University of Texas at El Paso has expanded its dining choices to encompass a me
has expanded its dining choices to encompass a meal plan option. Meal plans combine the feel of a fast-casual restaurant with the affordability and choice you have come to expect from UTEP Food Servi

hoice you have come to expect from UTEP Food Services. The meal plan permits you to enjoy a variety of foods that are fresh, made daily and all-you-care-to-eat. The Pick 'n Shovel is open daily, Monda

re-to-eat. The Pick 'n Shovel is open daily, Monday thru Friday 10:00am - 2:30pm. The Pick 'n Shovel is an all-you-can-eat buffet and has a rotating menu with comfort food favorites, international cui

enu with comfort food favorites, international cuisine, and a salad bar. Beverages are included. There are vegetarian and vegan options, as well as gluten and allergen-free foods. Meal Plan Terms: Mea
uten and allergen-free foods. Meal Plan Terms: Meal Plans cannot be cancelled, changed, or refunded, after census day of each semester. Students may cancel ONE (1) meal plan per semester . Meal Plans

ancel ONE (1) meal plan per semester . Meal Plans are to be used by the student only, no guests. Meal Plans for students purchased in fall will roll over to spring. Meal Plans, associated dollars, and

ver to spring. Meal Plans, associated dollars, and Flex Dollars, expire the last day of the spring semester. Meal Plans are nontransferable. Purchase of a meal plan signifies your understanding of, an

of a meal plan signifies your understanding of, and agreement to these terms. Faculty and Staff The Meal Plan for Faculty and Staff does roll over from semester to semester, is nonrefundable, and is a

Miner Meals UTEP Business Affairs Meal Plans UTEP Business Affairs Meal Plans Miner Meals Miner Meals is a program that offers a 10 percent discount at participating food venues on campus. Funds in Mi

t participating food venues on campus. Funds in Miner Meals can be deposited online with a minimum deposit of $5.00. There is no need to renew Miner Meals balances, as they roll over each semester. Ho

eals balances, as they roll over each semester. However, the Miner Gold Card will automatically deactivate when you graduate or separate from the University. Features Get a 10% discount at participati

ersity. Features Get a 10% discount at participating food venues on campus. Funds in Miner Meals can be deposited online with a minimum deposit of $5.00 or with cash or check at the cashiers in the Mi

00 or with cash or check at the cashiers in the Mike Loya Academic Services Building. Miner Meals balances roll over each semester. How to add funds: There are two easy ways to add money to your Miner

There are two easy ways to add money to your Miner Meals. 1. In Person: Bring cash or a check to the Cashiers located in the Mike Loya Academic Services Building. 2. Online: Visit https://get.cbord.co

TEP, consider trying some of these restaurants! Not only do they offer fantastic food, but they’re also a great way to meet other people in your area. Crust Pizza Co. Average rating: 4.9 stars out of
Crust Pizza Co. Average rating: 4.9 stars out of 5 stars. Find it at: 2300 Woodforest Pkwy N. This casual eatery offers delicious Italian-American fare at affordable prices. From their tasty subs, ca

re at affordable prices. From their tasty subs, calzones, and pizza to the incredible salads and pasta, you’ll never run out of something new to try. Locals are big fans of the casual setting and swee

rage rating: 4.4 stars out of 5 stars. Find it at: 2400 N Mesa St STE G. Rated the best sushi restaurant in El Paso , you don’t have to worry about paying too much for delicious contemporary Japanese
ying too much for delicious contemporary Japanese dishes. This is an awesome restaurant that leans into the Japanese atmosphere and gives you a nearly-authentic chance to try some of the best food in

-authentic chance to try some of the best food in the state. This is a great spot for a first date! Rib Hut Average rating: 4.5 stars out of 5 stars. Find it at: 2612 N Mesa St. Texas barbecue is some

Find it at: 2612 N Mesa St. Texas barbecue is something everyone should try at least once while they live here! El Paso’s best barbecue place is Rub Hut. offering everything from ribs to brisket and c

ut. offering everything from ribs to brisket and chicken-fried steak; this restaurant doesn’t try to be fancy and instead focuses on great flavor. Eating here once is enough to drive visitors to start

ing here once is enough to drive visitors to start looking for El Paso houses for sale . After your first bite, you may want to become a local too! Kaedama Average rating: 4.5 stars out of 5 stars. Fi

edama Average rating: 4.5 stars out of 5 stars. Find it at: 204 Boston Ave. If you want authentic ramen in a laid-back environment with tons of options: you’ll love it here. Although it’s closed every

s: you’ll love it here. Although it’s closed every Sunday, locals say this restaurant is the best of both worlds: cheap and incredibly delicious. This is great for a quick dine-in meal or for somethin
is great for a quick dine-in meal or for something more in-depth for anyone wanting to splurge on a ton of sides and options. Crave Kitchen and Bar Average rating: 4.3 stars out of 5 stars. Find it a

verage rating: 4.3 stars out of 5 stars. Find it at: 300 Cincinnati Ave. This brick-walled American bistro restaurant has upscale comfort food for all meals, and offers some of the best food you’ll ev
meals, and offers some of the best food you’ll ever taste. Everyone who eats here raves about it, saying the burgers and breakfast items are the best you can find in El Paso. Another incredibly affor
you can find in El Paso. Another incredibly affordable restaurant, you can’t go amiss by eating at the Crave Kitchen and Bar ! There Are Plenty of Great Eateries Here Whether you’re in the mood for p

FAQ - Food Pantry FREQUENTLY ASKED QUESTIONS Frequently Asked Questions What do I need to pick up food at the Food Pantry? Be an active student, faculty and staff member and bring your physical or dig

ty and staff member and bring your physical or digital UTEP ID. How can I help support the Food Pantry? Volunteer Donate Monetary Gift What donations are accepted? We accept all non-perishable food an

are accepted? We accept all non-perishable food and hygiene products. Food Items Canned vegetables Canned meat Canned soup Canned beans Canned fruit Shelf stable milk Juice Granola bars Pop tarts Oatm

helf stable milk Juice Granola bars Pop tarts Oatmeal Dry rice Dry spaghetti Dry beans Nuts/trail mix Cereal Pasta sauce Tomato sauce Peanut butter Jelly Pancake mix Hygiene Products Shampoo Condition

lly Pancake mix Hygiene Products Shampoo Conditioner Body wash/bar soap Body lotion Deodorant Toothpaste Pads Tampon How does it work? All food is free for eligible students/employees. Visitors may ch

e for eligible students/employees. Visitors may choose what they would like to take and quantities handed out depend on current availability. Food drives are conducted every semester and donations are

ves are conducted every semester and donations are always welcomed to ensure the pantry is as fully stocked as possible. There are two options for taking food from the pantry. Visitors may take a day'

ing food from the pantry. Visitors may take a day's worth of items (five single serving items) and visit up to twice a week OR they may take an estimated week's worth, and visit up to twice a month. Q

ted week's worth, and visit up to twice a month. Quantities handed out will depend on availability and will fluctuate, pantry staff will hand out items according to availability to keep the pantry sus

s according to availability to keep the pantry sustainable. Who is Eligible? UTEP undergraduate and graduate students who are enrolled in courses in fall or spring semester are eligible to use the Foo

all or spring semester are eligible to use the Food Pantry. Students do not need to be enrolled in summer classes in order to access the food pantry throughout the summer. Students must bring their Mi

hroughout the summer. Students must bring their Miner Gold Card each time they visit the food pantry. Current UTEP faculty and staff are also eligible to use the Food Pantry. Faculty and staff must be
to use the Food Pantry. Faculty and staff must be currently employed in order to access the food pantry. Faculty and Staff must bring their Miner Gold Card each time they visit the food pantry.
d Card each time they visit the food pantry.
